biography.title: Commissioner Biography
biography.summary: Dr. A. Amalraj IPS assumed charge as the Commissioner of Greater Chennai Police on May 21, 2026. With over 28 years of unblemished service in the Indian Police Service, ADGP Amalraj brings a legacy of security audits, modernization, and community-centered initiatives to the city's command.
biography.name: Dr. A. Amalraj IPS
biography.para1: Dr. A. Amalraj is a senior Indian Police Service (IPS) officer of the 1996 batch. Hailing from the Kanniyakumari district of Tamil Nadu, his administrative philosophy integrates scientific analytical methods, modern human resource practices, and proactive community engagement.
biography.para2: His academic credentials include a BSc and MSc in Physics, followed by an MBA in Human Resource Management, and a PhD from Madurai Kamaraj University. He has spent three decades serving across various district, commissionerate, and state-level divisions in Tamil Nadu, including leadership roles in Coimbatore, Salem, Trichy, and Tambaram.
biography.academicDesc: PhD from Madurai Kamaraj University, Physics MSc, and HR MBA.
biography.serviceDesc: Recipient of President’s Police Medal & CM’s Commendation.
